(**paramorphine**), also known as **codeine methyl enol ether**, is an [opiate](/wiki/Opiate "Opiate") [alkaloid](/wiki/Alkaloid "Alkaloid"), its name coming from the [Greek](/wiki/Greek_language "Greek language") Θῆβαι, *[Thēbai](/wiki/Thebes,_Egypt "Thebes, Egypt")* (Thebes), an ancient city in [Upper Egypt](/wiki/Upper_Egypt "Upper Egypt"). A minor constituent of [opium](/wiki/Opium "Opium"), thebaine is chemically similar to both [morphine](/wiki/Morphine "Morphine") and [codeine](/wiki/Codeine "Codeine"), but has stimulatory rather than depressant effects. At high doses, it causes convulsions similar to [strychnine](/wiki/Strychnine "Strychnine") poisoning. The synthetic [enantiomer](/wiki/Enantiomer "Enantiomer") (+)-thebaine does show analgesic effects apparently mediated through opioid receptors, unlike the inactive natural enantiomer (−)-thebaine.[[3]](#cite_note-aceto_1999-3) While thebaine is not used therapeutically, it is the main alkaloid extracted from *[Papaver bracteatum](/wiki/Papaver_bracteatum "Papaver bracteatum")* (Iranian opium / Persian poppy) and can be converted industrially into a variety of compounds that do have medicinal value.[[4]](#cite_note-World2015-4)

Chemical compound

**Thebaine** (**paramorphine**), also known as **codeine methyl enol ether**, is an [opiate](/wiki/Opiate "Opiate") [alkaloid](/wiki/Alkaloid "Alkaloid"), its name coming from the [Greek](/wiki/Greek_language "Greek language") Θῆβαι, *[Thēbai](/wiki/Thebes,_Egypt "Thebes, Egypt")* (Thebes), an ancient city in [Upper Egypt](/wiki/Upper_Egypt "Upper Egypt"). A minor constituent of [opium](/wiki/Opium "Opium"), thebaine is chemically similar to both [morphine](/wiki/Morphine "Morphine") and [codeine](/wiki/Codeine "Codeine"), but has stimulatory rather than depressant effects. At high doses, it causes convulsions similar to [strychnine](/wiki/Strychnine "Strychnine") poisoning. The synthetic [enantiomer](/wiki/Enantiomer "Enantiomer") (+)-thebaine does show analgesic effects apparently mediated through opioid receptors, unlike the inactive natural enantiomer (−)-thebaine.[[3]](#cite_note-aceto_1999-3) While thebaine is not used therapeutically, it is the main alkaloid extracted from *[Papaver bracteatum](/wiki/Papaver_bracteatum "Papaver bracteatum")* (Iranian opium / Persian poppy) and can be converted industrially into a variety of compounds that do have medicinal value.[[4]](#cite_note-World2015-4)

## Biosynthesis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thebaine&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biosynthesis")]

The biosynthesis of thebaine starts with the amino acid [tyrosine](/wiki/Tyrosine "Tyrosine") and proceeds via [reticuline](/wiki/Reticuline "Reticuline") in a pathway leading to many [benzylisoquinoline alkaloids](/wiki/Benzylisoquinoline_alkaloids "Benzylisoquinoline alkaloids").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-Ullmann-6) The final step is catalysed by the enzyme [thebaine synthase](/wiki/Thebaine_synthase "Thebaine synthase"), which forms a [2,3-dihydrofuran](/wiki/2,3-dihydrofuran "2,3-dihydrofuran") ring with loss of a molecule of acetic acid from 7-O-acetylsalutaridinol.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The reaction occurs spontaneously in alkali but requires catalysis to occur rapidly under physiological conditions near pH 7.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-NPR-9)

### Research

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thebaine&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Research")]

Thebaine has been produced by [genetically modified](/wiki/GMO "GMO") *[E. coli](/wiki/E._coli "E. coli")*.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Metabolism in the opium poppy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thebaine&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Metabolism in the opium poppy")]

In *[Papaver somniferum](/wiki/Papaver_somniferum "Papaver somniferum")*, there are two possible pathways to [morphine](/wiki/Morphine "Morphine") from thebaine. These differ according to which [methyl group](/wiki/Methyl_group "Methyl group") in thebaine is first to be removed in an [oxidation reaction](/wiki/Redox "Redox").

### Via neopinone

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thebaine&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Via neopinone")]

If the [enol ether](/wiki/Enol_ether "Enol ether") in thebaine is converted to a [ketone](/wiki/Ketone "Ketone") by [thebaine 6-O-demethylase](/wiki/Thebaine_6-O-demethylase "Thebaine 6-O-demethylase"), a methyl group leaves as [formaldehyde](/wiki/Formaldehyde "Formaldehyde"), with the oxidation driven by [molecular oxygen](/wiki/Molecular_oxygen "Molecular oxygen") activated as a [ferryl](/wiki/Ferryl "Ferryl") group Fe(IV)=O.[[9]](#cite_note-NPR-9)

[![2D representation of the chemical structure of thebaine .](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1f/Thebaine_skeletal.svg/250px-Thebaine_skeletal.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Thebaine_skeletal.svg)

+ [H2C=O](/wiki/Formaldehyde "Formaldehyde")

The alkaloid product of the reaction, neopinone, can spontaneously [isomerise](/wiki/Isomer "Isomer") to the next intermediate, codeinone, in the pathway to morphine via [codeine](/wiki/Codeine "Codeine") but this step is catalysed in opium poppy by the enzyme [neopinone isomerase](/w/index.php?title=Neopinone_isomerase&action=edit&redlink=1 "Neopinone isomerase (page does not exist)") (NISO).[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[6]](#cite_note-Ullmann-6)[[9]](#cite_note-NPR-9)

### Via oripavine

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thebaine&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Via oripavine")]

The alternative sequence to morphine begins when the enzyme [codeine 3-O-demethylase](/wiki/Codeine_3-O-demethylase "Codeine 3-O-demethylase") removes the methyl group from the [phenolic](/wiki/Phenol "Phenol") oxygen, giving oripavine. Further transformations convert this to morphinone and finally morphine.[[6]](#cite_note-Ullmann-6)[[9]](#cite_note-NPR-9)

[morphine](/wiki/Morphine "Morphine")

## As controlled drug

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thebaine&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: As controlled drug")]

Thebaine is controlled under international law, is listed as a Class A drug under the [Misuse of Drugs Act 1971](/wiki/Misuse_of_Drugs_Act_1971 "Misuse of Drugs Act 1971") in the United Kingdom, is controlled as an analog of a Schedule II drug per the [Analog Act](/wiki/Analog_Act "Analog Act") in the United States, and is controlled with its derivatives and salts, as a Schedule I substance of the [Controlled Drugs and Substances Act](/wiki/Controlled_Drugs_and_Substances_Act "Controlled Drugs and Substances Act") in Canada.[[12]](#cite_note-12) The 2013 US [Drug Enforcement Administration](/wiki/Drug_Enforcement_Administration "Drug Enforcement Administration") (DEA) aggregate manufacturing quota for thebaine (ACSCN 9333) was unchanged from the previous year at 145 metric tons.

## Global production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thebaine&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Global production")]

In 2012, 146,000 kilograms of thebaine were produced.[[13]](#cite_note-UN2015-13) In 2013, Australia was the main producer of [poppy straw](/wiki/Poppy_straw "Poppy straw") rich in thebaine, followed by Spain and then France. By 2017, worldwide thebaine production dropped to 2,008 kg.[[14]](#cite_note-14) Together, those three countries accounted for about 99 per cent of global production of such poppy straw. The seed capsules of *[Papaver bracteatum](/wiki/Papaver_bracteatum "Papaver bracteatum")* are the primary source of thebaine, with the stem additionally yielding a significant amount.[[4]](#cite_note-World2015-4)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

Although thebaine is not used therapeutically, it is a starting material for industrial productions of pharmaceutical drugs including [hydrocodone](/wiki/Hydrocodone "Hydrocodone"), [hydromorphone](/wiki/Hydromorphone "Hydromorphone"), [oxycodone](/wiki/Oxycodone "Oxycodone"), [oxymorphone](/wiki/Oxymorphone "Oxymorphone"), [nalbuphine](/wiki/Nalbuphine "Nalbuphine"), [naloxone](/wiki/Naloxone "Naloxone"), [naltrexone](/wiki/Naltrexone "Naltrexone"), [buprenorphine](/wiki/Buprenorphine "Buprenorphine"), [butorphanol](/wiki/Butorphanol "Butorphanol") and [etorphine](/wiki/Etorphine "Etorphine").[[4]](#cite_note-World2015-4)

## Adverse effects

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thebaine&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Adverse effects")]

*[The Canberra Times](/wiki/The_Canberra_Times "The Canberra Times")* of 16 November 2022[[16]](#cite_note-16) reported that four batches of Hoyts brand [poppy seeds](/wiki/Poppy_seeds "Poppy seeds") were being recalled due to unusually high levels of thebaine, and that at least twelve people in [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales") had required medical attention after ingesting them. As of 15 November 2022, [Food Standards Australia New Zealand](/wiki/Food_Standards_Australia_New_Zealand "Food Standards Australia New Zealand") (FSANZ) is coordinating a national recall of a number of poppy seed products due to the potential presence of thebaine.[[17]](#cite_note-17)
